description: A chain of ifs, gathered into a single knot.

hints.text: This is the reverse of exportation — **importation** (or uncurrying). $A \to B \to C$ is just $A \land B \to C$ written the long way. You're gathering a chain of [implication|implications] into a single [conjunction|conjunction] premise. Start by assuming $A \to B \to C$.
You're now proving $A \land B \to C$ under that [assumption|assumption]. Assume $A \land B$ — the knotted pair.
Feed $A$ to $A \to B \to C$ (step 1) with [modus-ponens|modus ponens] to get $B \to C$.
[modus-ponens|Modus ponens] again — this time $B \to C$ (step 5) and $B$ (step 4) — to reach $C$.
[assumption|Discharge] the original chain to finish. Knot tied.
